title: Error in podman: loading seccomp profile (/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json) failed: seccomp not enabled in this build".
body:
### Issue Description

I setup podman 4.5 using below https://www.computingpost.com/how-to-install-podman-4-x-on-centos-7-rhel-7/. But now container is not running

Some error for this podman version
"Error: loading seccomp profile (/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json) failed: seccomp not enabled in this build".

Can someone help here?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

2.

Describe the results you received

comments.body: Looks like you did not build podman with seccomp support?

> Looks like you did not build podman with seccomp support?

@rhatdan How is that done? I am not aware on it yet

> have you used `make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"`?
@giuseppe I had tried but then found that there was some error. Is this compulsory?

What error?  when building?

We don't really test with seccomp disabled, so I am not sure if the code totally handles seccomp not built in correctly.  Podman should not be handing seccomp.json to the oci runtime when not built with seccomp, is probably a bug, but not that important.

What platform are you attempting to build on?
you need to make sure seccomp is not referenced anywhere, e.g. the seccomp profile path must be empty.
I think containers.conf is going to default to this path if not set, so you might need to set the path to ""
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This is not really an upstream issue, so closing.
